blocks.body: Radical terms with the same radicand (number under the radical) and the same index. Like terms: 3 sqrt(5) and 7 sqrt(5). Unlike: 3 sqrt(2) and 3 sqrt(5).
Only like radicals can be added or subtracted. Combine coefficients and keep the radical. Example: 3 sqrt(7) + 5 sqrt(7) = 8 sqrt(7). 9 sqrt(2) - 4 sqrt(2) = 5 sqrt(2).
Always simplify radicals first, then check for like terms. Example: sqrt(12) + sqrt(27) = 2 sqrt(3) + 3 sqrt(3) = 5 sqrt(3).

blocks.label: Example
blocks.body: 2 sqrt(18) - sqrt(8) + 3 sqrt(50). Simplify: 6 sqrt(2) - 2 sqrt(2) + 15 sqrt(2) = 19 sqrt(2).
sqrt(3) + sqrt(5) cannot be simplified — they are unlike radicals. Do not confuse radical addition with multiplication: sqrt(3) x sqrt(5) = sqrt(15).
